title: Parse Disk Usage Report
description: Parse the output of the 'df -h' command and identify filesystems that are above a usage percentage threshold. Return a list of (mount_point, use_percent) tuples for filesystems exceeding the threshold, sorted by use_percent descending.
constraints: Input is df -h output; the first line is the header and must be skipped
Use% column is the 5th field (4th index), mount point is the 6th field
Return empty list if no filesystems exceed the threshold
Percent sign in the Use% column should be stripped when converting to int

examples.input:
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sda1        50G   45G    5G  90% /
/dev/sda2        20G    2G   18G  10% /boot
/dev/sda3       100G   85G   15G  85% /data
examples.explanation: / is at 90% and /data is at 85%, both above the 80% threshold. /boot at 10% is excluded. Results are sorted by percentage descending.

starter_code.python:
def high_disk_usage(df_output: str, threshold: int = 80) -> list[tuple[str, int]]:
    pass

test_cases.input:
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sda1        50G   45G    5G  90% /
/dev/sda2        20G    2G   18G  10% /boot
/dev/sda3       100G   85G   15G  85% /data

test_cases.description: All filesystems below threshold returns empty list
test_cases.input:
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sda1        50G    5G   45G  10% /

test_cases.description: Custom threshold of 50%, one filesystem exceeds it
test_cases.input:
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sda1       100G   60G   40G  60% /
/dev/sda2       100G   40G   60G  40% /tmp
